perturbation.original_text: The Recipient's obligations to protect, safeguard and handle Proprietary Information received hereunder shall not be binding if such Proprietary Information (i) is in the public domain through no fault of the Recipient, (ii) is known to the Recipient prior to disclosure hereunder, (iii) is lawfully disclosed to Recipient by a third party. The Recipient's obligations shall survive until the Proprietary Information has been returned, destroyed in accordance with written instructions or covered by a subsequent agreement between CELERITY and UNISYS for the contemplated services.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient's obligations to protect, safeguard and handle Proprietary Information received hereunder shall not be binding if such Proprietary Information (i) is in the public domain through no fault of the Recipient, (ii) is known to the Recipient prior to disclosure hereunder, (iii) is lawfully disclosed to Recipient by a third party. The Recipient's obligations shall terminate immediately upon the termination of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "The Recipient's obligations shall survive until the Proprietary Information has been returned, destroyed in accordance with written instructions or covered by a subsequent agreement between CELERITY and UNISYS for the contemplated services." to "The Recipient's obligations shall terminate immediately upon the termination of this agreement." in Section 5, the clause creates a contradiction with Section 4, which states: "Termination of this Agreement shall not be construed as relieving the Recipient of its obligations to safeguard, protect and dispose of the Proprietary Information as set forth in this Agreement." Now there is a contradiction as to when and if Proprietary Information needs to be safegaurded once the contract is terminated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. All Proprietary Information exchanged under this Agreement shall be returned or destroyed in accordance with written instructions upon the earlier of a written request from the Transmitter. Either party may terminate this Agreement, only with mutual consent and 90 days written notice. Termination of this Agreement shall not be construed as relieving the Recipient of its obligations to safeguard, protect and dispose of the Proprietary Information as set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 5 to state that the Recipient's obligations terminate immediately upon the termination of the agreement. This directly contradicts Section 4, which states that termination of the agreement does not relieve the Recipient of its obligations to safeguard, protect, and dispose of Proprietary Information. This creates a clear and direct conflict regarding the Recipient's responsibilities concerning Proprietary Information after the agreement's termination, satisfying the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 12. INVOICES/PAYMENTS SELLER shall submit an original invoice and two (2) copies to: UNISYS Corporation Attention: Frederick W. Garner III 8008 Westpark Drive, Mail Stop W5F39 McLean, Virginia 22102 SELLER's invoice must include: (l) Name and address of the SELLER, (2) Invoice Date (3) Prime Contract Number, contract line item number and purchase order number (4) Description, quantity, unit of measure, unit price and extended price of the items delivered. (5) Shipping number and date of shipment including the bill of lading number and weight of shipment. Shipping charges shall be itemized separately. (6) Terms of any prompt payment discount if offered. (7) Name and address of official to whom payment is to be sent; and (8) Name, title and phone number of person to be notified in event of defective invoice. SELLER shall submit invoices for payment for the Products and Services accepted under this Agreement. Invoices for Products and Services may be submitted after the date of delivery of such Products and/or Services to the F.O.B. destination site. Upon receipt of a proper invoice, BUYER agrees to pay SELLER for accepted Products and Services net forty-five (45) days.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. INVOICES/PAYMENTS SELLER shall submit an original invoice and two (2) copies to: UNISYS Corporation Attention: Frederick W. Garner III 8008 Westpark Drive, Mail Stop W5F39 McLean, Virginia 22102 SELLER's invoice must include: (l) Name and address of the SELLER, (2) Invoice Date (3) Prime Contract Number, contract line item number and purchase order number (4) Description, quantity, unit of measure, unit price and extended price of the items delivered. (5) Shipping number and date of shipment including the bill of lading number and weight of shipment. Shipping charges shall be itemized separately. (6) Terms of any prompt payment discount if offered. (7) Name and address of official to whom payment is to be sent; and (8) Name, title and phone number of person to be notified in event of defective invoice. SELLER shall submit invoices for payment for the Products and Services accepted under this Agreement. Invoices for Products and Services may be submitted after the date of delivery of such Products and/or Services to the F.O.B. destination site. Upon receipt of a proper invoice, BUYER may or may not pay SELLER for accepted Products and Services based on BUYER's discretion.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "BUYER agrees to pay SELLER for accepted Products and Services net forty-five (45) days." to "BUYER may or may not pay SELLER for accepted Products and Services based on BUYER's discretion." within Section 12, it creates an in-text contradiction. Initially, the BUYER is bound to pay in 45 days, but with the change the BUYER can choose when/if to pay. The uncertainty of payment obligations is created.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. INVOICES/PAYMENTS SELLER shall submit an original invoice and two (2) copies to: UNISYS Corporation Attention: Frederick W. Garner III 8008 Westpark Drive, Mail Stop W5F39 McLean, Virginia 22102 SELLER's invoice must include: (l) Name and address of the SELLER, (2) Invoice Date (3) Prime Contract Number, contract line item number and purchase order number (4) Description, quantity, unit of measure, unit price and extended price of the items delivered. (5) Shipping number and date of shipment including the bill of lading number and weight of shipment. Shipping charges shall be itemized separately. (6) Terms of any prompt payment discount if offered. (7) Name and address of official to whom payment is to be sent; and (8) Name, title and phone number of person to be notified in event of defective invoice. SELLER shall submit invoices for payment for the Products and Services accepted under this Agreement. Invoices for Products and Services may be submitted after the date of delivery of such Products and/or Services to the F.O.B. destination site. Upon receipt of a proper invoice, BUYER may or may not pay SELLER for accepted Products and Services based on BUYER's discretion.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the BUYER's obligation from a definite payment within 45 days to a discretionary payment ('may or may not pay'). This creates a direct contradiction within Section 12, as the original clause mandates payment terms whereas the changed clause introduces uncertainty, thus fulfilling the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 17. WARRANTY SELLER warrants and implies that the items delivered hereunder are merchantable and fit for use for the particular purpose described in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 17. WARRANTY SELLER warrants and implies that the items delivered hereunder are merchantable and fit for use for the particular purpose described in this Agreement. No warranty is offered by the SELLER.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "No warranty is offered by the SELLER." at the end of Section 17, a contradiction is created. The beginning of the section states that the seller provides a warranty, while the addition states that the seller gives no warranties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 17. WARRANTY SELLER warrants and implies that the items delivered hereunder are merchantable and fit for use for the particular purpose described in this Agreement. No warranty is offered by the SELLER.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 17 introduces a direct contradiction. The first part of the section states that the SELLER provides a warranty, while the added sentence explicitly states "No warranty is offered by the SELLER." This is a clear and direct conflict, making the warranty terms unenforceable and creating a structural flaw as defined by the perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: 8.0 BINDING ARBITRATION Any dispute or controversy between the BUYER and SELLER which concerns only the BUYER and the SELLER or which does not involve a final decision of the Government Contracting Officer, and which cannot be resolved by mutual agreement of the parties hereto, shall be settled by arbitration in accordance with the commercial rules then in effect of the American Arbitration Association. The place of such arbitration shall be Washington, D.C. Each party shall select one arbitrator and the two arbitrators so selected shall select the third arbitrator. The arbitrators shall be knowledgeable in Government procurement matters related to the types of supplies and services provided pursuant to this Agreement. The arbitration decision and award shall be binding on the parties, and judgment thereon may be entered in any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.0 BINDING ARBITRATION Any dispute or controversy between the BUYER and SELLER which concerns only the BUYER and the SELLER or which does not involve a final decision of the Government Contracting Officer, and which cannot be resolved by mutual agreement of the parties hereto, shall be settled by arbitration in accordance with the commercial rules then in effect of the American Arbitration Association. The place of such arbitration shall be Washington, D.C. Each party shall select one arbitrator and the two arbitrators so selected shall select the third arbitrator. The arbitrators shall be knowledgeable in Government procurement matters related to the types of supplies and services provided pursuant to this Agreement. The arbitration decision and award shall not be binding on the parties.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "The arbitration decision and award shall be binding on the parties, and judgment thereon may be entered in any court of competent jurisdiction." to "The arbitration decision and award shall not be binding on the parties." withing Section 8, it now contradicts its first part, where its stating that the arbitration shall be settled in accordance with the American Arbitration Association but not binding.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.0 BINDING ARBITRATION Any dispute or controversy between the BUYER and SELLER which concerns only the BUYER and the SELLER or which does not involve a final decision of the Government Contracting Officer, and which cannot be resolved by mutual agreement of the parties hereto, shall be settled by arbitration in accordance with the commercial rules then in effect of the American Arbitration Association. The place of such arbitration shall be Washington, D.C. Each party shall select one arbitrator and the two arbitrators so selected shall select the third arbitrator. The arbitrators shall be knowledgeable in Government procurement matters related to the types of supplies and services provided pursuant to this Agreement. The arbitration decision and award shall not be binding on the parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 8.0 of the agreement. The original text states that the arbitration decision is binding, implying a legally enforceable outcome. The changed text explicitly states that the arbitration decision is 'not binding'. This creates a direct contradiction because Section 8.0 now simultaneously indicates that arbitration is both binding (through reference to the American Arbitration Association) and not binding, making the clause internally inconsistent and unenforceable. This satisfies the definition of a structural flaw leading to an in-text contradiction.
